confirmed.logic: keep finite coordinates and non-null object IDs
confirmed.reason: required to identify the ECDFS footprint and preserve object-level joins
already used in `src/validate_dp1_against_hst.py` before morphology interpretation

confirmed.logic: for any band used, require finite and positive PSF/CModel fluxes
confirmed.reason: needed for physically meaningful flux-to-mag conversion and PSF-CModel morphology diagnostics

confirmed.logic: for any band used, keep rows where the corresponding flux flags are `False`

confirmed.logic: when using `*_extendedness` or `*_sizeExtendedness`, keep rows where the corresponding flag is `False`
confirmed.reason: the current notebook extendedness add-on explicitly excludes flagged values before plotting/comparison

likely.logic: for the first-look `g-i` vs `i` CMD, use rows with valid `g` and `i` CModel fluxes and clean CModel flux flags
likely.reason: this is the minimum defensible band-specific clean subset for a coadd-style CMD using `g-i` color and `i` magnitude
likely.evidence: consistent with the project requested CMD plus existing local flux-based workflow

likely.logic: for later pS-related morphology work, require valid PSF and CModel fluxes in the band(s) being modeled, at minimum `r`, or preferably `g/r/i` together for legacy `dm3`-style work
likely.reason: the repo's star/galaxy morphology code uses `*_diff`, which is undefined unless both fluxes are usable
